purpose: External research citations used to calibrate and validate EduPredict forecasts

enrollment_research.title: AI Index Report 2024 -- Chapter 6: Education
key_findings: AI-related postsecondary degree programs tripled since 2017 -- steady annual increase
AI/ML is most popular PhD specialty, comprising more than a quarter of all doctoral degrees awarded
CS master's and PhD graduate numbers slightly declined since 2018, remaining relatively flat
In 2022, AI PhD graduates entering industry: 70.7% (up from 40.9% in 2011) -- brain drain from academia
Industry-to-academia AI faculty transition dropped: 13% in 2019 to 7% in 2022
CS education becoming less international -- proportionally fewer international grads in 2022 vs 2021
enrollment_research.relevance: Confirms AI degree programs are growing; validates demand but flags supply-side competition

enrollment_research.title: Taulbee Survey 2024 -- Annual Report
key_findings: Master's degree production 'ballooned' in 2022-23 -- more than double the previous year's report
Record number of CS master's graduates in history of the Taulbee Survey
Total doctoral enrollment increased 4.7% across all responding departments
AI/ML is the most popular area of specialty for new PhDs (>25% of all PhDs where area is known)
57.5% of 2022-23 PhD recipients took positions in North American industry
enrollment_research.relevance: Strong evidence for MS-level AI program demand; master's enrollment spike validates EduPredict's optimistic scenario

enrollment_research.title: GradCafe Grad Trends: Interest in AI Surges
key_findings: Applications to AI-related programs have more than doubled
AI programs now represent ~0.3% of all graduate applications, up from <0.1%
Sustained upward trend through 2023 with notable spikes in 2016 and 2018
Graduate program naming shifting from 'machine learning' toward 'artificial intelligence'
enrollment_research.relevance: Direct evidence of application demand surge for AI graduate programs

enrollment_research.title: GMAC Application Trends Survey 2024
key_findings: 78% of business schools have integrated AI into their learning experiences
Most institutions still lack formal AI policies in admissions processes
enrollment_research.relevance: Shows AI integration in grad education is mainstream; creates differentiation opportunity

international_student_research.title: Fall 2024 Snapshot on International Student Enrollment
key_findings: International graduate students in U.S. hit record high in 2023/24: 502,000 students (+8%)
2024/25 new graduate enrollments fell 15% (geopolitical and visa uncertainty)
OPT participation record: 294,000 participants in 2024/25 (+21% YoY)
88% of international students cite post-study work opportunities as top priority
India top sending country: graduate enrollment +19% in 2023/24
Over 70% of students from India, Iran, Bangladesh, Nepal pursued STEM via OPT
international_student_research.relevance: International student pool is large and STEM-oriented; OPT growth signals strong AI program demand; 2024-25 dip is temporary

job_market_research.title: State of the Tech Workforce 2026
key_findings: U.S. tech employment forecast +1.9% in 2026 = 185,499 new jobs, total ~9.8 million workers
Tech occupations will grow at 2x the rate of overall U.S. employment over next decade
Data scientists and analysts: projected +420% over next 10 years
Cybersecurity analysts and engineers: projected +346% over next 10 years
Software developers and engineers: projected +188% over next 10 years
New York projected to add 13,566 tech jobs in 2026 (4th nationally)
NYC metro leads all U.S. metros: +11,317 tech jobs in 2026
275,000+ active job postings referenced AI skills in January 2026
Tech industry = 8.7% of U.S. GDP (~$2.3 trillion)
job_market_research.relevance: Real projected job numbers for NY; confirms extreme demand for data scientists and cybersecurity roles

job_market_research.title: LinkedIn Jobs on the Rise 2026 -- AI Roles
key_findings: AI Engineer is #1 fastest-growing job overall
AI roles make up 3 of the top 5 fastest-growing positions
AI job hiring concentrated in: San Francisco, NYC, Boston, Dallas
Top in-demand skills: LangChain, RAG, PyTorch, TensorFlow, MLOps, Computer Vision, Python
Growth in AI Consultant/Strategist and AI Researcher roles alongside technical roles
job_market_research.relevance: Boston (MA) is explicitly named as a top AI hiring hub -- validates MA multiplier; confirms skill alignment with AI programs

job_market_research.title: Labor Market Impacts of AI: A New Measure and Early Evidence
key_findings: Computer & Math occupations: 94% theoretical AI feasibility, only 33% actual coverage -- 61% gap
For every 10pp increase in AI coverage, BLS growth projections drop 0.6pp
Young worker (age 22-25) hiring in exposed occupations down 14%
Computer Programmers: 0.75 observed exposure (highest in tech)
Data Scientists: 0.65 observed exposure
High-exposure workers earn 47% salary premium above average
job_market_research.relevance: Core research integrated into job_market.py model; explains why AI skills are premium even as disruption risk grows

state_policy_research.title: Connecticut Tech Talent Accelerator 3.0 (TTA 3.0)
state_policy_research.source: Office of Governor Ned Lamont, State of Connecticut
key_findings: Since August 2024: ~11,000 CT job postings required AI skills -- 40% YoY increase
1 in 52 jobs in CT now requires AI skills (up from 1 in 70 a year ago)
For roles requiring associate/bachelor's degree: 1 in 23 jobs requires AI expertise
7 CT institutions launched AI programs in Feb 2026 pilot (TTA 3.0)
University of New Haven: launching 'AI for Cybersecurity' concentration in BS Cybersecurity
University of Connecticut: cross-disciplinary AI workflow agent microcredential
Southern Connecticut State: AI in Manufacturing + quantum computing microcredential
12 business partners co-developing curricula including CGI, Accenture, others
Second round of grants planned for mid-2026 for new industry-led AI programs
state_policy_research.relevance: DIRECT RELEVANCE -- UNH is one of the 7 institutions; CT government validates AI program demand; AI for Cybersecurity is the exact program in EduPredict

roi_research.title: AI Master's Degree Salary and ROI Analysis 2026
key_findings: Entry-level AI professionals: $95,000 median starting salary
Mid-career AI (5-9 years): $140,000 median
Senior AI roles: $165,000-$220,000
ML Product Engineers avg: $186,152
Average payback period for AI degrees: 4.2 years
85% of AI graduates find employment within 6 months of graduation
Most AI master's programs show positive ROI within 1-2 years
Program cost ranges: public in-state $50K, out-of-state $112K, private $180K-$280K
roi_research.relevance: Validates EduPredict ROI model; confirms starting salary assumptions; gives professor proof of program viability

roi_research.title: Master's Programs Are Cash Cows for Universities
key_findings: CS/Engineering master's programs: break-even within 1-3 years
20-year net return: $450,000-$520,000 for CS/Engineering master's
Master's programs are significant revenue sources for universities
roi_research.relevance: Validates the university revenue model in EduPredict; confirms 3.43x ROI is realistic

data_quality_notes.enrollment_data: IPEDS EF Survey -- federal law requires all Title IV institutions to report; data is verified and audited
data_quality_notes.salary_data: BLS OES May 2023 -- mandatory employer survey, ~1.1M establishments sampled
data_quality_notes.job_market_data: CompTIA 2026 + BLS Employment Projections 2022-2032 -- annually revised government data
data_quality_notes.research_citations: Peer-reviewed + government + major industry research organizations only
